title: Bitbucket の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article provides generally accurate factual claims regarding Bitbucket 401 errors and their causes.
However, the primary solutions detailed in the article heavily rely on 'App Passwords', which are a deprecated authentication method for Bitbucket Cloud.

As of the current date (June 15, 2026), App Passwords have either already ceased functioning (June 9, 2026) or will be permanently removed soon (July 28, 2026). This renders a significant portion of the article's troubleshooting steps obsolete.
The stated default expiration for OAuth access tokens (2 hours) is slightly inconsistent with some sources indicating 1 hour.
One claim regarding a locked account as a cause for a 401 error lacks direct official Bitbucket grounding in the provided search results.
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: **URGENT:** Rewrite all sections mentioning 'App Passwords' to reflect their deprecation and instead recommend 'API tokens' or SSH keys as the current best practice for authentication. Provide updated instructions for generating and using API tokens.
Clarify the expiration period for OAuth access tokens by referencing the latest official documentation, addressing any discrepancies (e.g., between 1 and 2 hours).
Provide a specific Bitbucket official source to support the claim about an account being locked as a cause for a 401 error, or remove/rephrase the claim.
Add a prominent disclaimer at the beginning of the article highlighting the deprecation of App Passwords and the importance of migrating to API tokens.
Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: App password permissions | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: App passwords are single purpose, user-based access tokens which can be created with limited permissions (or scope). Bitbucket Cloud allows for repository permission levels like Read, Write, Admin, and Delete.

sources.title: Bitbucket API Access token expiry - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Bitbucket OAuth Tokens have an expiry time of 1 hour after which a refresh token is needed to refresh the access token.

sources.title: Enhancing security in Bitbucket: Introducing expiry for access tokens - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: Workspace admins can configure access token expiry policies (e.g., 90, 180, or 365 days) or allow no expiry, and users must set an expiration within these limits when creating new tokens.

sources.title: Bitbucket app password or access token - AWS CodeBuild
sources.claim: Bitbucket app passwords or access tokens require specific permission scopes (e.g., repository:read) for access to various resources.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud support - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Official Bitbucket Cloud support is available for documentation, knowledge base, community, system status, and bug reports.

sources.title: Getting 401 HTTP error when using Basic authentication with Bitbucket Cloud REST APIs
sources.claim: A 401 error indicates incorrect credentials. When using an app password for authentication with Bitbucket Cloud APIs, the username (not the Atlassian account email) must be used. API tokens are the recommended approach for basic authentication, using the Atlassian account email.

sources.title: API request failed with status 401. Check your account and app password and try again.
sources.claim: Receiving a 401 error usually means credentials are invalid or permissions are lacking, and it's important to use the Bitbucket username (not email) with app passwords.

sources.title: Authentication problem - Bitbucket - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: Clearing stored credentials from Git Credential Manager on Windows or Keychain Access on macOS can resolve authentication issues, as old cached credentials may interfere.

sources.title: Require access token expiry | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Workspace admins can set a maximum expiry date range (e.g., 365 days) for access tokens created within a workspace, project, or repository.

sources.title: How to Add an SSH Key to Bitbucket and Clone Your Repository Securely (Step-by-Step Guide) | by Shivam Garud | Medium
sources.claim: Using SSH keys for Git operations with Bitbucket eliminates password prompts, provides higher security through encryption, and is ideal for automation and CI/CD pipelines.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud resources - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Cloud provides resources for getting started with version control, setting up and working on repositories, and managing branches and pull requests.

sources.title: BitBucket OAuth2.0 access for web application - HiBit
sources.claim: Bitbucket OAuth access tokens typically expire in two hours, after which 401 responses will be received, and a refresh token is used to obtain a new access token.

sources.title: Two 401 responses for every Git operation | Bitbucket Data Center
sources.claim: Git operations over HTTP(S) can result in an initial 401 response from Bitbucket, as Git first sends only the username, prompting the server to request the password before re-sending the request with full credentials.

sources.title: You can now sign commits with SSH keys! - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: Bitbucket Cloud now supports using SSH keys to sign commits, enhancing security and traceability.

sources.title: Setting up SSH Keys for Github, GitLab, and Bitbucket in one windows pc - DEV Community
sources.claim: SSH key authentication is more secure and convenient than HTTPS with username/password, as it uses encryption and avoids repeated credential entry for Git operations.

sources.title: How to retrieve Bitbucket OAuth token information? - API - Latenode Official Community
sources.claim: Bitbucket OAuth tokens generally last for an hour, requiring a refresh token to obtain a new access token when expired.

sources.title: Using SSH keys to secure Git operations | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Cloud allows users to add personal SSH keys to their account for secure Git operations, enabling authentication without storing user credentials.

sources.title: BitBucket App Password | GitGuardian documentation
sources.claim: App passwords are user-based access tokens with limited permissions that can be revoked from Bitbucket settings.

sources.title: Enable two-step verification | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: When two-step verification is enabled, applications and Git clients must use SSH or an app password to access Bitbucket Cloud, as basic username/password authentication will not work.

sources.title: Bitbucket Authentication, 2FA and OAuth | Git-Credential-Manager-for-Windows
sources.claim: If 2FA is enabled, username/password Basic Auth access to Bitbucket REST APIs and Git repositories is suspended, requiring username/app-specific-password Basic Auth, OAuth, or SSH.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud transitions to API tokens: enhancing security with app password deprecation - Inside Atlassian
sources.claim: App Passwords are being deprecated and will cease to function by June 9, 2026 (full deprecation), with new App Password creation disabled from September 9, 2025. Users are encouraged to transition to API tokens for authentication.

sources.title: How to remove credentials from Git? - MindStick
sources.claim: On Windows, Git credentials can be removed via the Credential Manager by expanding Windows Credentials, locating the Git entry, and selecting Delete.

sources.title: Using App passwords | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: App passwords are being deprecated and will be permanently removed by July 28, 2026; all integrations should switch to API tokens immediately. To authenticate with App passwords, the Bitbucket username (not email or app password label) is required.

sources.title: Do I have to use app passwords? : r/bitbucket - Reddit
sources.claim: If multi-factor authentication or SSO is used, an app password or SSH key is often required because most Git clients cannot use a single username and password.

sources.title: Bitbucket Cloud Knowledge Base - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: The Bitbucket Cloud Knowledge Base provides troubleshooting articles and support resources.

sources.title: 401 error on Git push (Bitbucket) when the remote url includes just the username · Issue #1942 - GitHub
sources.claim: A 401 error can occur during Git push to Bitbucket if the authorization header is incorrectly changed from 'Bearer' to 'Basic' with only a username and empty password.

sources.title: Bitbucket Integration Fails in Terraform Enterprise with Unauthorized Error
sources.claim: A 401 Unauthorized response indicates incorrect credentials or insufficient permissions; for example, Bitbucket integration failures can be caused by lacking webhook management or pull request write access.

sources.title: 2FA for Bitbucket: U2F & TOTP - Alpha Serve
sources.claim: Two-factor authentication (2FA) for Bitbucket requires an authenticator app to scan a QR code and generate a verification code, which is then entered to enable 2FA.

sources.title: Bitbucket Introduces Required Two-Factor Authentication and IP Whitelisting - InfoQ
sources.claim: Bitbucket offers mandatory two-factor authentication for teams, where users attempting to access their account without 2FA enabled will be denied access and shown instructions.

sources.title: Managing and Removing Git Credentials Securely - GeeksforGeeks
sources.claim: On Windows, Git Credential Manager can be used to remove Git-related credentials from the Windows Credentials section of the Control Panel.

sources.title: Updating credentials from the macOS Keychain - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: On macOS, saved Git credentials in the `git-credential-osxkeychain` helper can be updated or deleted via Keychain Access or using command line tools like `git credential-osxkeychain erase`.

sources.title: Update your username | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Changing a Bitbucket username affects the username used for basic authentication, including cloning a repository over HTTPS.

sources.title: How do I set up git-credential-manager-core in Windows to use bitbucket with oauth?
sources.claim: To clear cached credentials in Windows, navigate to Windows Credential Manager and delete any Bitbucket-related entries.

sources.title: I keep getting a 401 error when running my script how do I fix this - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: For Bitbucket API calls or Git operations via HTTPS, the Bitbucket username and an app password with appropriate permissions must be used, not an email address and account password.

sources.title: Bitbucket Integration Issue - Authentication Error (401)
sources.claim: An 'Invalid personal access token' message in the UI or a 401 'Unauthorized' error in logs indicates an authentication problem with Bitbucket integration.

sources.title: usename vs email - Bitbucket - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: When configuring a Bitbucket account in SourceTree with basic authentication, the username (as defined in Bitbucket settings) should be used, not the email address.

sources.title: How to remove a bitbucket account from a MAC - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: On macOS, Bitbucket account credentials can be removed from the Keychain Access application.

sources.title: Configure SSH and two-step verification | Bitbucket Cloud - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: Bitbucket Cloud supports four encryption algorithms for SSH client keys (Ed25519, ECDSA, RSA, and DSA) and accepts public keys in OpenSSH's authorized_keys format.

sources.title: Navigating Bitbucket Cloud and Atlassian Two-Step Verification for Bitbucket Cloud Users
sources.claim: Atlassian Two-Step Verification (2SV) provides an additional security layer for the entire Atlassian account, including Bitbucket Cloud, by requiring an extra verification factor.

sources.title: Connecting to Bitbucket | Appcircle Docs
sources.claim: Bitbucket has replaced App Passwords with API Tokens, although existing App Passwords can still be used until mid-2026.

sources.title: Atlassian Cloud Support - Digital Marketplace
sources.claim: Atlassian Cloud Support offers professional assistance for Atlassian Cloud products like Bitbucket.

sources.title: Can't login to bitbucket server accout when username is an email - Atlassian Community
sources.claim: When trying to log into Bitbucket Server with an email address as the username for basic authentication, it can result in a 'NotFound' error because the email is not recognized as a username.

sources.title: Delete a keychain in Keychain Access on Mac - Apple Support
sources.claim: Keychains can be deleted via the Keychain Access app on Mac by selecting a keychain and choosing 'File > Delete Keychain'.

sources.title: How do you reset the stored credentials in 'git credential-osxkeychain'? - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: On Mac, Git credentials can be reset using the command `git credential-osxkeychain erase` or by manually removing entries from the Keychain Access application.

unsupported_claims: アカウント自体がロックされている可能性もあります。
